request.question: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The intervention and biological system are specified, but the comparator/control and numeric thresholds are missing.
clarifying_questions: What is the control condition for comparison?
What is the numeric threshold for increased cytotoxicity?
What are the details of the mechanistic rationale for the increase in cytotoxicity?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: CRISPR TP53 knockout iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes doxorubicin cytotoxicity LDH
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: CRISPR knockout of TP53 in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes will increase doxorubicin-induced cytotoxicity measured by LDH release

references.title: Cellular and electrophysiological characterization of triadin knockout syndrome using induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cardiomyocytes
references.relevance_note: Paper discusses CRISPR-Cas9 application in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, relevant to the intervention and biological system.

references.title: Applications of genome editing technology in the targeted therapy of human diseases: mechanisms, advances and prospects
references.relevance_note: This paper provides a background on genome editing technologies and their applications in diseases, relevant to understanding the context of the intervention.

references.title: TAB2 deficiency induces dilated cardiomyopathy by promoting mitochondrial calcium overload in human iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes
references.relevance_note: Investigates mechanisms in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, which may provide related insights into cellular mechanisms relevant to cytotoxicity.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of CRISPR knockout is represented in paper [3], which discusses CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, although it focuses on a different gene (TRDN) than TP53. The biological system of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes is also present in multiple papers, indicating relevant experimental context. However, none of the papers specifically measure cytotoxicity in relation to LDH release or directly address TP53 knockout, which leaves the exact parameters of the hypothesis unfulfilled, thus classifying the work as similar rather than exact.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: Determine the cytotoxicity of TP53 knockout iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes measured by LDH release compared to control cells.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment involves generating TP53 knockout iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes through CRISPR-Cas9 technology, followed by measuring cytotoxicity using an LDH assay. The results will be compared against wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes to ascertain the effect of TP53 knockout on cell viability.
controls: Normal iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes treated with 0.5% Triton X-100 to induce maximal LDH release.
Wild-type iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes in non-cytotoxic conditions (no treatment).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Maintenance
Thaw iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes (iCell Cardiomyocytes, Fujifilm) in a 37°C water bath until just thawed (approx. 2 min).
Expected outcome: Viable cardiomyocytes ready for culture.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Cell Culture Maintenance
Transfer thawed cardiomyocytes to a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL of pre-warmed cardiomyocyte maintenance medium (CM, CAT#: MABT-001, Merck) and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Pelleted cardiomyocytes for resuspension.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cell Culture Maintenance
Resuspend the pelleted cardiomyocytes in 2 mL of cardiomyocyte maintenance medium and plate in a 24-well plate at a density of 5 × 10^5 cells/well (1 mL per well).
Expected outcome: Cardiomyocytes properly seeded in 24-well plates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | CRISPR Design
Design sgRNA targeting TP53 using an online CRISPR design tool and order two unique sgRNAs for improved knockout efficiency.
Expected outcome: Two sgRNA sequences for TP53 obtained.

DAY 1 | 20 min | CRISPR Transfection
Prepare lipofectamine 2000 transfection mixture by combining 2 µg of each sgRNA with 4 µL of lipofectamine in 100 µL of Opti-MEM for 20 min at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Transfection complex ready for application.

DAY 1 | 5 min | CRISPR Transfection
Replace maintenance medium in the 24-well plates with 500 µL of fresh cardiomyocyte maintenance medium and add 100 µL of the transfection mixture to each well containing cardiomyocytes.
Expected outcome: Transfection of cardiomyocytes initiated.

DAY 1 | 24 h | CRISPR Transfection
Incubate transfected cardiomyocytes for 24 h in an incubator at 37°C with 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Transfected cardiomyocytes cultured.

DAY 2 | 5 min | Media Change
After 24 h, replace the medium with 1 mL of cardiomyocyte maintenance medium to remove the transfection reagent.
Expected outcome: Transfection medium replaced.

DAY 3 | 15 min | Cell Lysis for HDR
Harvest cardiomyocytes for HDR analysis at 48 h post-transfection by washing cells with 1 mL of PBS and lysing with 100 µL of RIPA buffer for 10 min on ice.
Expected outcome: Cell lysate collected for HDR quantification.

DAY 3 | 2 h | HDR Confirmation
Perform PCR for HDR confirmation using 1 µL of lysate as template with specific primers for TP53 knockout.
Expected outcome: PCR amplicon indicating successful TP53 knockout.

DAY 4 | 10 min | Cell Viability Assay
Detach cardiomyocytes by adding 200 µL of trypsin-EDTA per well for 5 min at 37°C, and then neutralize with 800 µL of complete cardiomyocyte maintenance medium.
Expected outcome: Single-cell suspension of cardiomyocytes.

DAY 4 | 15 min | Cytotoxicity Assay
Prepare LDH assay according to the kit manufacturer (e.g., LDH Cytotoxicity Assay Kit, Thermo Fisher): Add 100 µL of cardiomyocyte suspension to assay wells in a 96-well plate.
Expected outcome: LDH assay wells prepared for readings.

DAY 4 | 30 min | Cytotoxicity Assay
Add 100 µL of the reaction mixture from the LDH kit to each well of cardiomyocytes in the 96-well plate and incubate at room temperature for 30 min.
Expected outcome: Reaction mixture incubated to develop color.

DAY 4 | 10 min | Cytotoxicity Assay
Measure the absorbance at 490 nm using a plate reader (e.g., BioTek Synergy HT) to quantify LDH release.
Expected outcome: Absorbance values for LDH quantification obtained.

DAY 4 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze LDH release data using GraphPad Prism® software to compare TP53 knockout versus wild-type cardiomyocytes, applying unpaired t-test for significance with a p-value threshold of <0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance assessed for cytotoxicity levels.

DAY 4 | 30 min | Data Interpretation
Interpret results in relation to cytotoxicity and summarize findings including LDH release differences and biological implications.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive report on the effects of TP53 knockout.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: CRISPR-Cas9 technology effectively generates TP53 knockout in iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes.
LDH release is a reliable measure of cytotoxicity in cardiomyocytes.
iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes maintain functional viability during culture and treatment.
decision_criteria: Cytotoxicity assessed by LDH release should be significantly different (p < 0.05) between TP53 knockout and wild-type cells.
LDH levels in positive control should exceed 150% of wild-type levels.
Cell viability in negative control should remain above 90%.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes for all experiments.
Standardize the sgRNA and transfection conditions across trials.
Perform PCR with the same thermal cycler settings for HDR analysis.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: (numeric target TBD) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
